changes_summary: Revised plan to address both flags: (1) Added Step 2 with clip-to-generation resolution via asset registry, shot lookup via shot_generations table, and multi-anchor shot creation — solving the gap where the plan couldn't resolve clips to generations/shots. (2) Added image-to-video as in-scope via the existing travel_between_images resolver, changed tool schema to use reference_image_urls array (not singular), and added i2v dispatch path in the generation tool that maps to the travel_between_images family with image_urls/base_prompts/segment_frames/frame_overlap. Updated assumptions to remove the i2v out-of-scope claim.
questions: The travel_between_images resolver has many optional fields (motion settings, phase config, lora settings, etc.). For the agent's i2v path, using defaults (segment_frames=[49], frame_overlap=[2], model_name from resolver defaults) should be sufficient for an MVP. Should we expose any of these as agent-controllable parameters, or keep it simple?

When the agent creates a shot for results, should it also pass the shot_id to the task so generated outputs automatically link to that shot via the DB trigger, or should linking happen separately after generation completes?

success_criteria.criterion: Agent can create style-transfer tasks using selected clip URLs as reference images via image_generation family with reference_mode=style
Agent can create subject-transfer tasks using selected clip URLs via reference_mode=subject
Agent can create scene-transfer tasks using selected clip URLs via reference_mode=scene
Agent can create image-to-video tasks using 2+ selected clip URLs via travel_between_images family
Basic text-to-image generation (no reference) still works via the agent
Task creation uses the family/input API format expected by create-task edge function
Selected clip URLs are resolved to generation IDs via the asset registry for shot operations
Agent checks if selected images already share a shot before creating a new one
Agent can create a new shot and link multiple anchor generations to it
System prompt explains when and how to use each task type with examples
Existing timeline editing commands (move, trim, delete, etc.) are unaffected
create_task tool has structured JSON parameters with reference_image_urls as array
New tool integrates with existing agent loop (tool_call/tool_result turn format, error handling)
Old generate command still works for backward compatibility
Supabase edge function deployed and tested with real task creation

assumptions: The create-task edge function requires family/input format — the old task_type/params format is broken. Will fix as part of Step 1.
Style/subject/scene transfer use the image_generation family with reference_mode — no new resolvers needed.
Image-to-video uses the existing travel_between_images family resolver. The agent will pass sensible defaults for segment_frames ([49]) and frame_overlap ([2]) — advanced tuning is out of scope.
Default to qwen-image model when reference images are used, since only qwen-image and z-image support reference params.
Shot creation is conditional: only create a new shot when anchors don't already share one AND the user/context warrants it.
The create_task tool is a separate structured tool alongside run, not a text command.
The agent already receives selected clip URLs via selected_clips payload — no frontend changes needed.
The asset registry loaded in loadTimelineState contains generationId on asset entries, which is sufficient to resolve clips to generations without additional DB queries.
The SupabaseAdmin type in the agent needs minor extension to support insert and multi-eq queries for shot_generations.
